articles.title: Nimble Way raises $47M to give AI agents access to real-time web data
articles.summary: Nimble uses AI agents to search the web, verify and validate the results, and then clean and structure the information into neat tables that can then be queried like a database.

articles.title: Inside Anthropic’s existential negotiations with the Pentagon
articles.summary: Anthropic's weekslong battle with the Department of Defense has played out over social media posts, admonishing public statements, and direct quotes from unnamed Pentagon officials to the news media. But the future of the $380 billion AI startup comes down to just three words: "any lawful use." The new terms, which OpenAI and xAI have reportedly already agreed to, would give the US military carte blanche to use services for mass surveillance and lethal autonomous weapons, AI that has full power…

articles.title: AI Will Never Be Conscious
articles.summary: In his new book, A World Appears, Michael Pollan argues that artificial intelligence can do many things—it just can’t be a person.

articles.title: We’re putting more stuff into space than ever. Here’s what’s up there.
articles.summary: Earth’s a medium-size rock with some water on top, enveloped by gases that keep everything that lives here alive. Just at the edge of that envelope begins a thin but dense layer of human-built, high-tech stuff. People started putting gear up there in 1957, and now it’s a real habit. Telescopes look up and out…

articles.title: Why conservationists are making rhinos radioactive
articles.summary: Every year, poachers shoot hundreds of rhinos, fishing crews haul millions of sharks out of protected seas, and smugglers carry countless animals and plants across borders. This illegal activity is incredibly hard to disrupt, since it’s backed by sophisticated criminal networks and the perpetrators know that their chances of being caught are slim. With an…

articles.title: Canva acquires startups working on animation and marketing
articles.summary: With the new acquisitions, the company wants to bolster its position as a marketing solution by potentially adding video creation and more granular measurement.

articles.title: A Meta AI security researcher said an OpenClaw agent ran amok on her inbox
articles.summary: The viral X post from an AI security researcher reads like satire. But it's really a word of warning about what can go wrong when handing tasks to an AI agent.

articles.title: With AI, investor loyalty is (almost) dead: At least a dozen OpenAI VCs now also back Anthropic
articles.summary: While some dual investors are understandable, others were more shocking, and signal the disregard of a longstanding ethical conflict-of-interest rule.

articles.title: Anthropic accuses DeepSeek and other Chinese firms of using Claude to train their AI
articles.summary: Anthropic claims DeepSeek and two other Chinese AI companies misused its Claude AI model in an attempt to improve their own products. In an announcement on Monday, Anthropic says the "industrial-scale campaigns" involved the creation of around 24,000 fraudulent accounts and more than 16 million exchanges with Claude, as reported earlier by The Wall Street Journal. The three companies - DeepSeek, MiniMax, and Moonshot - are accused of "distilling" Claude, or training a smaller AI model based on a…

articles.title: Anthropic accuses Chinese AI labs of mining Claude as US debates AI chip exports
articles.summary: Anthropic accuses DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax of using 24,000 fake accounts to distill Claude’s AI capabilities, as U.S. officials debate export controls aimed at slowing China’s AI progress.

articles.title: Uncanny Valley: AI Researchers’ Resignations, Bots Hiring Humans, Evie Magazine’s Party
articles.summary: This episode of Uncanny Valley covers the people resigning from AI companies and the humans getting hired by AI agents. Plus, we attend a soiree thrown by a conservative women's magazine.

articles.title: Google’s Cloud AI leads on the three frontiers of model capability
articles.summary: AI models are pushing against three frontiers at once: raw intelligence, response time, and a third quality you might call "extensibility."

articles.title: Peptides are everywhere. Here’s what you need to know.

articles.title: OpenAI calls in the consultants for its enterprise push
articles.summary: OpenAI is partnering with four consulting giants in an effort to see more adoption of its OpenAI Frontier AI agent platform.

articles.title: Guide Labs debuts a new kind of interpretable LLM
articles.summary: The company open sourced an 8-billion-parameter LLM, Steerling-8B, trained with a new architecture designed to make its actions easily interpretable.

articles.title: Particle’s AI news app listens to podcasts for interesting clips so you you don’t have to
articles.summary: AI news app Particle can now pull in key moments from podcasts, letting readers instantly play short, relevant clips alongside related stories.

articles.title: Does Big Tech actually care about fighting AI slop?

"Authenticity is becoming infinitely reproducible," Mosseri lamented. "Everything that made creators matter - the ability to be real, to connect, to have a voice that couldn't be faked - is now accessible to anyone with the right tools." But people,…

articles.title: How AI agents could destroy the economy
articles.summary: Citrini Research imagines a report from two years in the future, in which unemployment has doubled and the total value of the stock market has fallen by more than a third.

articles.title: Defense Secretary summons Anthropic’s Amodei over military use of Claude
articles.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has summoned Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to the Pentagon for a tense discussion over the military's use of Claude. Hegseth has threatened to designate Anthropic a "supply chain risk."

articles.title: How many AIs does it take to read a PDF?
articles.summary: Image: Kristen Radtke / The Verge Last November, the House Oversight Committee had just released 20,000 pages of documents from the estate of Jeffrey Epstein, and Luke Igel and some friends were clicking around, trying to follow the threads of conversation through garbled email threads and a PDF viewer that was, frankly, "gross." In the coming months, the Department of Justice would release its own batches of files, more than three million of them - again, all PDFs. This was a problem. While the…

articles.title: Inside Chicago’s surveillance panopticon

Four people were shot and killed on a westbound train as it approached the suburb of Forest Park. The police swiftly activated a digital dragnet—a surveillance network that connects thousands…

articles.title: Samsung is adding Perplexity to Galaxy AI
articles.summary: I wish I could talk to my Plex server… | Image: Samsung. In addition to summoning Bixby or Gemini, Galaxy S26 users will be able to call on Perplexity by saying "hey, Plex." The integration of Perplexity into Galaxy AI is just one element of the company's embrace of a "multi-agent ecosystem." Often, people will use different AI agents for different tasks, depending on where their strengths lie. So Samsung is opening up the ability to integrate different agents into the OS. Hey, Plex isn't just…

articles.title: All the important news from the ongoing India AI Impact Summit
articles.summary: India is hosting a four-day AI Summit this week that will be attended by executives from major AI labs and Big Tech, including OpenAI, Anthropic, Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, and Cloudflare, as well as heads of state.

articles.title: How to Hide Google’s AI Overviews From Your Search Results
articles.summary: You can avoid Google’s AI summaries in your search results by simply adjusting your query. Or just switch search engines altogether.

articles.title: Suspect in Tumbler Ridge school shooting described violent scenarios to ChatGPT
articles.summary: The suspect in the mass shooting at Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, Jesse Van Rootselaar, was raising alarms among employees at OpenAI months before the shooting took place. This past June, Jesse had conversations with ChatGPT involving descriptions of gun violence that triggered the chatbot's automated review system. Several employees raised concerns that her posts could be a precursor to real-world violence and encouraged company leaders to contact the authorities, but they ultimately…

articles.title: Trump is making coal plants even dirtier as AI demands more energy
articles.summary: Kingston Fossil Plant, a 1.4-gigawatt coal-fired power plant located in Roane County, just outside Kingston, Tennessee on the shore of Watts Bar Lake. | Photo: Getty Images The Trump administration just tossed out Biden-era restrictions on mercury and other toxic pollutants from power plants. It's repealing Mercury and Air Toxics Standards (MATS) just as electricity demand in the US ticks up with the buildout of new AI data centers. Those standards are particularly impactful when it comes to…

articles.title: AI Safety Meets the War Machine
articles.summary: Anthropic doesn’t want its AI used in autonomous weapons or government surveillance. Those carve-outs could cost it a major military contract.

articles.title: Amazon blames human employees for an AI coding agent’s mistake
articles.summary: Amazon Web Services suffered a 13-hour outage to one system in December as a result of its AI coding assistant Kiro's actions, according to the Financial Times. Numerous unnamed Amazon employees told the FT that AI agent Kiro was responsible for the December incident affecting an AWS service in parts of mainland China. People familiar with the matter said the tool chose to "delete and recreate the environment" it was working on, which caused the outage. While Kiro normally requires sign-off from…

articles.title: OpenAI’s first ChatGPT gadget could be a smart speaker with a camera
articles.summary: OpenAI's first hardware release will be a smart speaker with a camera that will probably cost between $200 and $300, according to The Information. The device will be able to recognize things like "items on a nearby table or conversations people are having in the vicinity," The Information says, and it will have a Face ID-like facial recognition system so that people can purchase things. OpenAI acquired Jony Ive's hardware company last May in a deal worth nearly $6.5 billion. Details about their…

articles.summary: Massive data centers for generative AI are bad for the Earth. How about launching them into orbit?

articles.title: Job titles of the future: Breast biomechanic
articles.summary: Twenty years ago, Joanna Wakefield-Scurr was having persistent pain in her breasts. Her doctor couldn’t diagnose the cause but said a good, supportive bra could help. A professor of biomechanics, Wakefield-Scurr thought she could do a little research and find a science-backed option. Two decades later, she’s still looking. Wakefield-Scurr now leads an 18-person team…

articles.title: The Pitt has a sharp take on AI
articles.summary: Each episode of HBO's The Pitt features some degree of medical trauma that almost makes the hospital drama feel like a horror series. Some patients are dealing with gnarly lacerations while others are fighting off vicious blood infections that could rob them of their limbs, and the chaos of working in an emergency room often leaves The Pitt's central characters shaken. But as alarming as many of The Pitt's more gore-forward moments can be, what's even more unsettling is the show's slow-burning…

articles.title: Code Metal Raises $125 Million to Rewrite the Defense Industry’s Code With AI
articles.summary: The Boston startup uses AI to translate and verify legacy software for defense contractors, arguing modernization can’t come at the cost of new bugs.

articles.title: Perplexity’s Retreat From Ads Signals a Bigger Strategic Shift
articles.summary: The AI search startup once predicted advertising would be a massive business. Now it's betting on a smaller, more valuable audience.

articles.title: This AI Tool Will Tell You to Stop Slacking Off
